title: Keyboard Shortcuts & Multitasking
description: Work smarter, not harder! Master the keyboard shortcuts that make Linux a joy to use.

steps.instruction: Let's start with the most iconic Linux shortcut: press Ctrl+Alt+T to open the Terminal!
steps.hint: Hold Ctrl and Alt together, then press T.

steps.instruction: Terminal opened! Now close it with Ctrl+W — this closes the current window.
steps.hint: Hold Ctrl and press W to close the focused window.

steps.instruction: Now use Ctrl+Shift+N to create a new folder on the desktop. This works everywhere in Linux!
steps.hint: Hold Ctrl and Shift together, then press N.

steps.instruction: You're a shortcut master! Open the Files app from the dock to continue.
steps.hint: Click the Files icon (📁) in the dock.
